title: CSS Grid Layout with Inconsistent Row Height Calculation and Variable Item Count

statement.markdown: Given a CSS grid container with dynamic row heights, implement a function `calculateGridRowHeights` that calculates the height of each row based on the number of items in the row. The grid container has a fixed height of 1000px. The function should return an array of row heights in the order they appear in the grid.
statement.inputFormat: A string containing the CSS grid container styles and a string containing the grid items.
statement.outputFormat: An array of integers representing the height of each row in the grid.
statement.notes: The grid container has a fixed height of 1000px. The grid items are represented as a string where each item is separated by a comma.

constraints.description: Maximum height of a single grid item

examples.input: grid-container { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); grid-template-rows: repeat(3, 1fr); grid-auto-columns: 1fr; grid-auto-rows: 1fr; } item1, item2, item3
examples.explanation: The grid container has 3 rows and 3 columns. Each row has 3 items, so the height of each row is 100px.

examples.input: grid-container { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); grid-template-rows: repeat(2, 1fr); grid-auto-columns: 1fr; grid-auto-rows: 1fr; } item1, item2, item3, item4
examples.explanation: The grid container has 2 rows and 2 columns. The first row has 2 items, so the height of the first row is 100px. The second row has 2 items, so the height of the second row is 300px.

hints.text: First hint text: Understand the CSS grid layout and how to calculate row heights.
Second hint text: Use the grid-template-rows and grid-auto-rows properties to determine the row heights.

starterCode.javascript: // Return an array of row heights in the order they appear in the grid.

functionSignature.python: def solve(self, gridStyles: str, gridItems: str) -> list[int]:

solutions.javascript: // Return an array of row heights in the order they appear in the grid.

testCases.input: grid-container { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); grid-template-rows: repeat(3, 1fr); grid-auto-columns: 1fr; grid-auto-rows: 1fr; } item1, item2, item3
grid-container { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); grid-template-rows: repeat(2, 1fr); grid-auto-columns: 1fr; grid-auto-rows: 1fr; } item1, item2, item3, item4
